title: Implement stable persistence storage for media manager

requirements.summary: Update MediaUploadZone to successfully upload images and PDFs through backend methods
acceptanceCriteria: File upload triggers backend uploadMediaAsset call
Upload progress is displayed to user
Success toast shown on successful upload
Error toast shown with descriptive message on failure

file_operations.description: Update the component to integrate with the blob-storage component for large file uploads. Use ExternalBlob.fromBytes() to handle file content and withUploadProgress() to track upload progress. Call the backend uploadMediaAsset method with the blob reference (assetId from ExternalBlob), filename, mimeType, assetType ('image' or 'document'), and fileSize. Verify the blob-storage component's usage instructions before implementing. Display upload progress percentage in the UI using a progress bar or indicator. Show success toast with asset ID on completion, and error toast with descriptive messages for validation failures (file type, size limits, token expiration). Ensure file type validation (JPEG, PNG, GIF, WebP, PDF) and size constraints (5MB for images, 10MB for PDFs) are enforced before upload.

Update the useUploadMediaAsset hook to integrate with the blob-storage component. Import ExternalBlob from the blob-storage module. Modify the upload mutation to first upload the file blob using ExternalBlob.fromBytes(fileContent).withUploadProgress(onProgress), retrieve the assetId/blobId from the uploaded blob, then call the backend uploadMediaAsset method with the blobId, filename, mimeType, assetType, and fileSize parameters. Handle authentication checks for valid delegation tokens before upload. Add proper error handling for blob upload failures and backend method failures. Return the asset ID on successful upload for use in success notifications.

requirements.summary: Update MediaAssetGrid to display uploaded media assets from backend with thumbnails and functional actions
acceptanceCriteria: Grid fetches and displays all media assets from backend
Images display as thumbnails using blob data
PDF files show with document icon
Copy ID button copies asset ID to clipboard
Delete button removes asset from backend and refreshes grid

file_operations.description: Update the component to integrate with the blob-storage component for displaying media assets. For each MediaAsset from getAllMediaAssets(), use the blobId field to create an ExternalBlob instance via ExternalBlob.fromURL() or retrieve blob bytes. Use ExternalBlob.getDirectURL() to get cached HTTP URLs for displaying image thumbnails in tags to leverage browser caching and streaming. For PDF assets (identified by mimeType starting with 'application/pdf'), display a document icon instead of attempting image preview. Verify the blob-storage component's usage instructions before implementing. Ensure the Copy ID button copies the asset's numeric ID (converted to string) to clipboard using navigator.clipboard.writeText(). Wire the Delete button to call the deleteMediaAsset backend method with proper authentication checks, show a confirmation dialog before deletion, and invalidate the 'mediaAssets' query key after successful deletion to refresh the grid. Display loading skeletons during initial fetch and show appropriate empty state when no assets exist.

Update the useGetAllMediaAssets hook to properly fetch media assets from the backend getAllMediaAssets method and transform the response for frontend consumption. Update the useGetMediaAssetById hook to fetch specific assets by ID using getMediaAssetById backend method and integrate with blob-storage to retrieve blob data via ExternalBlob if needed for preview purposes. Update the useDeleteMediaAsset hook to call the deleteMediaAsset backend method with authentication validation and proper error handling. Ensure all hooks return properly typed data matching the MediaAsset interface (id, filename, mimeType, blobId, size, uploadedAt, uploadedBy). Add network error classification and handle authentication errors (expired tokens) with descriptive error messages.
